title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Concurrent Registration
summary: Each user creates duplicate fetch jobs for the same feed, unfollow deletes shared data, ownership is ambiguous, and source verification happens outside a transaction. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go concurrent registration increment in shared-source and tenant subscription model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Use unique constraints and transaction retry to reconcile simultaneous equivalent source registrations.
Authorize source administration, subscription access, and moderation by tenant role and resource relationship in repositories.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Concurrent Registration · Theory Feed Go Concurrent Registr…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5944 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile shared source identity, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle, authorization, and registration races; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
